rationale: A batch script (C:\Temp\1.bat) executed via cmd.exe spawned reg.exe twice to write plaintext credentials — the Administrator username (A0, EV-001) and a password (EV-002, EV-003) — into the Winlogon AutoLogon registry keys, a well-known credential exposure and persistence technique. The password value was confirmed written to HKLM\...\Winlogon\DefaultPassword (EV-003). While EV-004 shows the DefaultPassword value was subsequently deleted by regedit.exe under a different process GUID (suggesting a cleanup step), the credential was still exposed in plaintext in the registry and in process telemetry during the window; the deletion does not negate the malicious intent or the credential exposure that occurred. The origin script in C:\Temp\ and the deliberate credential-staging pattern are inconsistent with legitimate administrative tooling, and no signed vendor context or change-management justification is present.
